skill.name: youtube-search-api-skill
skill.description: Extract structured data from YouTube search results using the BrowserAct API. Search for videos, shorts, channels, and playlists by keywords and retrieve titles, descriptions, view counts, publish dates, and URLs without manual browsing.
skill.summary: Search YouTube and extract structured result data via the BrowserAct API.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings were evaluated as false positives. The skill makes legitimate HTTP requests to the documented BrowserAct API endpoint and reads the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable for authentication, which is standard practice for third-party API integrations. Markdown backticks in SKILL.md were incorrectly flagged as shell command execution. No malicious intent was detected.

low_findings.title: Third-party API dependency
low_findings.description: The skill relies on the BrowserAct external API service. Users must provide a valid API key. Network availability and service terms affect functionality.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The API base URL is hardcoded and the skill cannot function without external network access to BrowserAct.

low_findings.title: Environment variable required for API key
low_findings.description: The script reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY from environment variables. This is standard practice but users must configure it before use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard and safe pattern for API key configuration. No exfiltration or unsafe handling detected.

content.user_title: Search YouTube and extract structured video data
content.value_statement: Manually copying YouTube search results is slow and error-prone. This skill automates structured data extraction via the BrowserAct API, delivering clean titles, descriptions, view counts, and URLs directly in your terminal.
actual_capabilities: Search YouTube by keywords and retrieve structured result lists
Filter results by type: Videos, Shorts, Channels, or Playlists
Extract title, description, view count, publish date, and URL for each result
Poll long-running browser automation tasks and report progress with timestamps
Handle API errors and authentication failures with clear user guidance
Limit result counts to match user-defined extraction needs
limitations: Requires a valid BrowserAct API key configured in the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable
Dependent on BrowserAct service availability and network connectivity
Execution may take several minutes due to browser automation overhead
Only returns data visible on the YouTube search results page

use_cases.title: Competitor video analysis
use_cases.description: Search for competitor brand names or product keywords on YouTube and extract top video metadata to analyze content strategies and engagement.

use_cases.title: Tutorial and educational content curation
use_cases.description: Find and compile structured lists of tutorial videos for specific tools or topics, complete with URLs and view counts, for learning resource pages.

use_cases.title: Trend monitoring for content creators
use_cases.description: Track YouTube Shorts and video search results for trending hashtags or niche keywords to identify emerging content opportunities.
use_cases.target_user: Content creators and social media managers

prompt_templates.title: Basic keyword search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search YouTube for videos about 'machine learning for beginners' and return the top 20 results with titles, URLs, and view counts.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner user performing a simple topic search

prompt_templates.title: Shorts trend discovery
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the latest YouTube Shorts for the keyword 'productivity tips' and extract the title, URL, and view count for each result.
prompt_templates.scenario: User focusing on short-form video trends

prompt_templates.title: Channel research for outreach
prompt_templates.prompt: Search YouTube Channels for ' indie game development' and collect channel names, URLs, and subscriber counts from the search results.
prompt_templates.scenario: User researching creators for collaboration or outreach

prompt_templates.title: Playlist curation for a topic
prompt_templates.prompt: Search YouTube Playlists for 'Python data science' and return playlist titles, URLs, and video counts. Limit to 10 playlists.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user curating structured learning resources

output_examples.input: Search YouTube for 'Claude Code tutorial' and return the top 5 videos.
output: Title: Getting Started with Claude Code - Full Tutorial
Title: Claude Code Tips and Tricks for Developers

output_examples.input: Find YouTube Shorts about 'morning routine' and extract metadata.
output_examples.output: The search returned 15 Shorts. Top result: 'My 5AM Morning Routine' with 890,000 views, published 1 week ago. URL: https://www.youtube.com/shorts/example1. All results include title, description snippet, view count, publish date, and direct URL.

best_practices: Always verify that the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable is set before running searches
Start with a small result limit to confirm the query returns relevant data before requesting large datasets
Monitor the terminal output for status logs during execution rather than interrupting long-running tasks
anti_patterns: Do not share your BrowserAct API key in public repositories or chat logs
Avoid setting excessively high result limits without a clear use case, as this increases execution time
Do not retry automatically when the output contains 'Invalid authorization'; instead, prompt the user to verify the API key

faq.question: Why do I need a BrowserAct API key?
faq.answer: The skill uses the BrowserAct API to run browser automation workflows that extract data from YouTube search pages. The API key authenticates your account and enables these requests.

faq.question: How do I get a BrowserAct API key?
faq.answer: Visit the BrowserAct Console at https://www.browseract.com/reception/integrations, sign in, and copy your API key. Then set it as the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable.

faq.question: How long does a search take to complete?
faq.answer: Execution time varies based on result count and BrowserAct queue status. Most tasks complete within 1 to 5 minutes. The script prints timestamped status updates while polling.

faq.question: What data fields are returned for each result?
faq.answer: Each result typically includes the title, description snippet, view count, publish date, and direct URL. The exact fields depend on what BrowserAct extracts from the YouTube search page.

faq.question: Can I search for content types other than videos?
faq.answer: Yes. The script supports Videos, Shorts, Channels, and Playlists. Specify the desired type as the second argument when calling the script.

faq.question: What should I do if the task fails?
faq.answer: If the output contains 'Invalid authorization', verify your API key and do not retry. For other errors, the skill will retry once automatically. If the second attempt fails, report the error message to the user.
